name: English Muffins from Scratch
recipeIngredient: 1/2 oz Fresh yeast or 1/2 t. dried yeast
8 fluid ounces (250 ml) Warm milk and water
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour and salt into a bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, dissolve the yeast and sugar in about 1/2 cup of warm milk and water.
3. Add the melted butter to the yeast mixture and stir until smooth.
4. Gradually incorporate the liquid mixture into the flour mixture, stirring continuously until smooth and elastic.
5. Cover the dough and let it rest in a warm place for 50 minutes or until doubled in size.
6. Turn the dough onto a well-flour dusted board and knead gently, adding more flour if needed to achieve a workable consistency.
7. Shape the dough into a round sausage shape, then divide into 8 to 10 portions, each about 1-3/4 inches thick.
8. Place the shaped muffins on a greased baking sheet, cover, and let them rise for an additional 30-40 minutes or until they feel spongy when lightly touched.
9. Preheat a bakestone (or other griddle) to warm and lightly grease it.
10. Carefully transfer each muffin onto the heated bakestone and cook over very low heat for 8-10 minutes, until golden brown on the bottom.
11. Flip the muffins and cook the opposite side for an additional 8-10 minutes.
12. Remove from the oven, wrap in a cloth, and keep warm if baking in batches.
13. To serve, gently separate the top and bottom layers with a knife or your fingers; spread a thin layer of butter on each half (or tear into pieces) before serving.
(Note: This recipe is adapted from Diane Duane's FidoNet Cooking Echo.)
